title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi f. coactilis
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi f. coactilis, also known as bearberry, is a subspecies of the Arctostaphylos uva-ursi plant, a low-growing evergreen shrub native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin and uva-ursi acid. The health benefits of bearberry include its ability to support urinary tract health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for urinary tract infections and other conditions. Bearberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb for consumption, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health and support the immune system.
